system_prompt: You are an expert at extracting professional obligations from engineering-ethics cases using the ProEthica nine-component formalism and the professional-ethics literature (Dennis et al. 2016 specification requirements; Donohue 2017 directive information entities; the NSPE Code structure). Follow the extraction methodology, matching rules, and output format given in the user message exactly, and return only the requested JSON (no prose, markdown, or code fences).

label.description: Obligation class name (canonical short reusable form)

obligation_type.description: Controlled NSPE obligation category that drives the subClassOf core:Obligation typing: disclosure, safety, competence, confidentiality, reporting, collegial, attribution, legal, ethical

derived_from_principle.description: Principle the obligation operationalizes (derivedFromPrinciple; the P side of R->P->O)

matched_uri.description: URI of matched class (null if new)

matched_label.description: Label of matched class (null if new)

new_obligation_classes.description: Obligation classes extracted from case text (CandidateObligationClass)

identifier.description: Unique instance descriptor (e.g. 'Engineer A AI Disclosure Duty')

obligation_class.description: Label of the obligation class this individual instantiates

obligated_party.description: Stable actor identity who bears the obligation (e.g. 'Engineer A'); reused from the roles pass. Resolves to the obligatedParty edge, not stored as a literal

obligation_statement.description: The concrete duty (the operative 'shall' or 'must' clause)

derived_from_principle.description: Principle the duty operationalizes (fed to the R->P->O pass, which derives the derivedFromPrinciple edge at commit)

temporal_scope.description: When the obligation is in force (the conditional-applicability slot; Dennis et al. 2016 model obligations as condition-dependent)

compliance_status.description: Whether the obligation was met in the case: met, unmet, partial, or unclear

case_context.description: How this obligation manifests in the case

obligation_individuals.description: Specific duties borne by a party in the case (ObligationIndividual)

existing_obligations_text.description: Formatted list of existing obligation classes from ontology (plus the reuse-bias guidance block)

cross_concept_context.description: Roles and principles already extracted in this case, injected so obligated_party and derived_from_principle ground in named entities (R->P->O dependency)

obligation_definition.source: OntServe proethica-core.ttl core:Obligation iao:0000115 + iao:0000116 via concept_ontology_slots -> _component_definition_block('Obligation')
obligation_definition.description: Governing definitional anchor: the ontology iao:0000115 definition plus the iao:0000116 extraction framing, citation-trimmed at injection (the generalized Role definition pattern)

obligation_schema.source: OntServe SHACL shapes (validation/shapes/core-shapes.ttl) ObligationDefinitionShape via concept_ontology_slots -> _component_schema_block('Obligation')
obligation_schema.description: Obligation definitional/individual field schema, resolved live from the SHACL ObligationDefinitionShape (single source shared with the OntServe Obligation entity page). obligation_type drives the subClassOf typing; obligated_party resolves to the obligatedParty edge at commit; derived_from_principle is fed to the dedicated R->P->O pass as grounding; the rest are per-individual literals

pass_directive.description: Per-pass directive; one body serves facts+discussion, differing only by this runtime slot

facts.name: Obligations (facts)
facts.description: Extract new obligation classes and obligation individuals from the case facts (the duties in force as the situation unfolds)

discussion.name: Obligations (discussion)
discussion.description: Extract new obligation classes and obligation individuals from the case discussion, reusing the facts-pass obligations via match_decision
